title: phanera coccinea subsp. tonkinensis
intro: Phanera coccinea subsp. tonkinensis, a type of flowering plant in the family Dipterocarpaceae, is a lesser-known species native to Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, derived from its roots and stems. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating pain, making it a potential natural remedy for various ailments. Phanera coccinea subsp. tonkinensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional medicine, though more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
